name: Monastery of Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular from Talamantes
name_original: Monasterio de Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular desde Talamantes
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Talamantes traverse a landscape characterized by the foothills of the Moncayo massif, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features a mix of rural roads, historical sites like monasteries, and elevated viewpoints. Cyclists can expect significant elevation changes, with routes often climbing through forested areas and open countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Talamantes The most popular touring cycling route is Moncayo — gravel circular from Monasterio de Veruela, a 28.2 miles (45.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbs and gravel sections. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monastery of Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular from Talamantes, a difficult 20.9 miles (33.7 km) path. This route offers a mix of ascents and descents, passing by historical landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Calcena and Purujosa Loop via the Hidden Face of Moncayo, a 15.6 miles (25.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Talamantes is defined by challenging climbs, historical monasteries, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options primarily for experienced cyclists, with a few moderate routes available. The routes in Talamantes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Talamantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes in Talamantes?
A: Talamantes is primarily known for its challenging touring cycling routes. While there are no routes classified as 'easy', you can find 4 routes of moderate difficulty. These routes still feature elevation changes but are less demanding than the 14 difficult routes available.

Q: What kind of elevation changes can I expect on touring cycling routes in Talamantes?
A: Touring cycling routes around Talamantes are characterized by significant elevation changes, as they traverse the foothills of the Moncayo massif. Many routes involve substantial climbs, often through forested areas and open countryside, offering rewarding views.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Talamantes?
A: Given the significant elevation changes and the prevalence of difficult routes (14 out of 18), Talamantes is generally more suited for experienced cyclists. There are no routes specifically classified as 'easy', so routes may not be ideal for young children or beginners.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Talamantes?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes in Talamantes are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993131894/moncayo-gravel-circular-from-monasterio-de-veruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncayo — gravel circular from Monasterio de Veruela&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994022959/monastery-of-santa-maria-de-veruela-y-ambel-circular-from-talamantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular from Talamantes&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied terrain and historical sights.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes around Talamantes?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant climbs and varied terrain. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Añón de Moncayo – Mirador de La Diezma Viewpoint loop from Vera de Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 53 km with nearly 1000 meters of ascent, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calcena – Calcena loop from Calcena&lt;/a&gt;, a 50 km route with over 770 meters of climbing.

Q: What historical sites can I visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in history. A notable historical site is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on several routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994022959/monastery-of-santa-maria-de-veruela-y-ambel-circular-from-talamantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de Veruela y Ambel — circular from Talamantes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Talamantes offers elevated viewpoints with panoramic vistas. You can find scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Peñas de Herrera&lt;/a&gt;, which provide stunning perspectives of the surrounding landscape and the Moncayo massif.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore near the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling, you can explore natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Pilares&lt;/a&gt;, a cave, or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5943916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soriano Water Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers a different kind of natural experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Talamantes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging climbs, the historical monasteries, and the panoramic viewpoints that define the region's touring cycling experience.

Q: Are there any less crowded touring cycling routes in Talamantes?
A: While specific crowd levels aren't tracked, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993364350/calcena-and-purujosa-loop-via-the-hidden-face-of-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calcena and Purujosa Loop via the Hidden Face of Moncayo&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through mountainous terrain, might offer a more secluded experience compared to routes closer to major historical sites.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Talamantes?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for significant elevation, spring and autumn are generally the most favorable seasons for touring cycling in Talamantes. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant, avoiding the intense heat of summer or the colder conditions of winter.
tips: The Talamantes castle was built in the 11th century with the purpose of defending the border crossing between Castile and Aragon in the Valdeherrera and Valdetreviño ravines. Although much of the fortification is in a state of ruin, the north wall and one of its towers are still quite well preserved.

We are in the monastery of Santa María de Veruela, a magical place in the province of Zaragoza. The monastery combines Romanesque, Gothic, Renaissance and Baroque architecture and is surrounded by a wall. We can also visit the cloister or the exhibition of the Bécquer brothers since they worked and stayed here. Admission is free for children up to 11 years old (€2 for adults) but it is totally worth it if we are interested in the whole story.
Also called Palacio de los Hospitalarios, it is a fortress located in the municipality of Ambel. On December 4, 2001, it was declared an Asset of Cultural Interest.
